auditor: Chartered Professional Accountants, Licensed Public Accountants
one_time_description: Ministry of the Attorney General (MAG) provincial transfer of $1,790,000 - appears to be a one-time payment not present in prior year
economic_notes: First Nation has investments in energy sector (Twin Falls LP - hydro electric project, Umbata Falls LP), forestry/mining/construction (BMI-Maamwigaaboo Contracting LP). Capacity funding from Williams Operating Corporation for economic development. Road maintenance contract with Nawiingnokiima Forest Management Corporation. COVID-19 impacts noted.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to investment in Twin Falls Limited Partnership ($4,172,416) - potential impairment indicators identified but full impact not assessed; financial statements for Twin Falls LP for year ended Dec 31, 2020 were not available at time of audit. Three consolidated subsidiaries: Biigtigong Dbenjgan Ltd., Begetekong Trust, MKWA Contracting GP Ltd. Three GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: Umbata Falls LP (51% owned), Twin Falls LP (97% owned, via Kagiano Power Corporation), BMI-Maamwigaaboo Contracting LP (48% owned). Umbata Falls LP has accumulated losses of $11,740,790 but made cash distributions of $815,918 to the First Nation during the year. Large one-time provincial MAG transfer of $1,790,000 not present in prior year. CEBA loan of $40,000 received with $10,000 forgivable portion recorded as government assistance revenue. Auditor firm name not clearly stated - identified only as Chartered Professional Accountants, Licensed Public Accountants based in Thunder Bay, Ontario. Schedule of remuneration not included in extracted text. Salaries total not separately disclosed in extracted text.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
